articles.headline: Four Indian Lenders Seek $2 Billion via Special RBI Swap
articles.summary: State Bank of India, Axis Bank, Bank of Baroda, and PFC are planning to raise at least $2 billion through External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs). They intend to utilize the Reserve Bank of India's 1.5% special fixed-rate swap window to mitigate currency risks. This initiative aims to take advantage of favorable global interest rates while maintaining domestic liquidity stability.

articles.headline: India Considers Resuming Iran Oil Imports Amid Trade Revival
articles.summary: India is studying the implications of a potential peace deal between the US and Iran to resume oil imports. New Delhi is monitoring the Hormuz Strait for safe passage of vessels, including 16 fertilizer ships currently stuck. The move is part of a broader strategy to utilize the International North-South Transport Corridor and secure energy supplies via the Chabahar port.

articles.headline: Government to Launch PLI Scheme for Lithium and Nickel Processing
articles.summary: The Indian government plans to introduce a Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme and duty concessions to boost domestic refining of critical minerals like lithium and nickel. The initiative aims to reduce dependence on overseas processors, particularly China, and build a ₹16,300 crore national mineral ecosystem to support the burgeoning electric vehicle and battery industry.

articles.headline: Cabinet Clears ₹19,700 Crore Carbon Capture Incentive Scheme
articles.summary: The Expenditure Finance Committee has approved a ₹19,700 crore scheme to promote Carbon Capture, Utilisation, and Storage (CCUS). The program aims to help heavy industries like power and steel reduce their carbon footprint. The government expects to attract private investments worth ₹37,500 crore to create a capture capacity of 7 million tonnes per year.

articles.headline: Centre Warns Ministries to Secure Data Against AI-Driven Threats
articles.summary: Following concerns over NEET-UG leaks and cyberattacks, the Home Ministry has directed all central ministries to review and strengthen their digital infrastructure. The advisory focuses on defending against AI-driven vulnerabilities and ensuring third-party vendors comply with strict security protocols. Ministries must conduct audits and set up response teams for real-time threat mitigation.

articles.headline: Sauce VC Exits Innovist with Massive 8-10x Returns
articles.summary: Consumer-focused Sauce VC has achieved a significant exit by selling its stake in Innovist to L’Oréal. The deal, valued at approximately ₹350-450 crore, marks one of the most successful early-stage exits in India’s D2C sector. The venture capital firm had initially invested about ₹10 crore in the startup, which owns brands like Bare Anatomy and SunScoop.

articles.headline: RIL Targets 40 Billion Units of Green Power Annually
articles.summary: Reliance Industries (RIL) announced its goal to generate 40 billion units of green electricity annually, representing nearly 3% of India’s current power requirement. The company is setting up a 40-gigawatt-hour battery giga-factory this year as part of its transition to a net-zero future. RIL plans to use proprietary technology to manufacture affordable solar modules and hydrogen.

articles.headline: BRICS Security Meeting to Discuss Post-Conflict Stability in Iran
articles.summary: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval is set to join BRICS counterparts to discuss regional security and the potential for stability in Iran following recent diplomatic shifts. The meeting, scheduled for June 23, will focus on counter-terrorism, maritime security, and building a consensus on non-traditional threats ahead of the main BRICS summit in September.

articles.headline: DGMO Channel with Pakistan Remains Functional Despite LAC Tensions
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel was informed that the Director General of Military Operations (DGMO) level communication channel between India and Pakistan remains active. Despite ongoing challenges on the Line of Actual Control (LAC) with China, the 2021 ceasefire agreement with Pakistan along the Line of Control (LoC) is largely holding, providing a mechanism for de-escalation.

articles.headline: TMC Files for Disqualification of 20 Rebel Members of Parliament
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) has formally approached the Lok Sabha Speaker seeking the disqualification of 20 rebel MPs who have shifted allegiance to other political factions. The party argues that these members have violated the anti-defection law. This move follows internal friction and significant shifts in regional political dynamics ahead of upcoming sessions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Dismisses Plea for CBI Probe into Tamil Nadu Trust
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has rejected a petition seeking a CBI investigation into alleged financial irregularities within a prominent Tamil Nadu state trust. The bench headed by the Chief Justice noted that the allegations were vague and lacked credible material evidence. The court emphasized that investigative agencies cannot be deployed based on unsubstantiated claims and political rivalry.

articles.headline: Karnataka Approves ₹7,506 Crore Industrial Investments, Creating 28,000 Jobs
articles.summary: The Karnataka state government has cleared 55 industrial projects worth ₹7,506 crore. Key investors include Kaynes Technology and Bellatrix Aerospace. These projects, spread across electronics, manufacturing, and ESDM sectors, are expected to generate over 28,000 jobs. A significant portion of these investments will be located in the Chamarajanagar and Kolar districts.

articles.headline: ED Raids Companies Over Alleged ₹2,500 Crore Crypto Money Laundering
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids on multiple entities in Bengaluru for alleged violations in cross-border money transfers involving crypto assets worth ₹2,500 crore. The investigation focuses on firms using virtual digital assets to bypass traditional banking channels and the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA). Several bank accounts have been frozen as part of the probe.

articles.headline: IHG Hotels Plans Expansion at Adani-Managed Airports in India
articles.summary: InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG) is in talks to expand its hotel portfolio by partnering with Adani Airports. The plan involves building high-end transit hotels and business properties near major airports like Mumbai and Ahmedabad. This move aligns with IHG's strategy to capture the growing demand for luxury airport-linked hospitality services in India’s Tier-1 cities.

articles.headline: Ray-Ban Meta AI Glasses Face Privacy Concerns During Gujarat Pilot
articles.summary: A pilot project using Meta's AI-powered Ray-Ban glasses for visually impaired users in Gujarat has sparked privacy debates. While the glasses help users navigate and identify objects, experts worry about the lack of consent from bystanders whose images are captured and processed by AI models. Legal activists are calling for stricter data protection guidelines for wearable cameras.

articles.headline: MNCs and Consultancy Firms Face New Taxes on Expat Salaries
articles.summary: High Court rulings have clarified that salary reimbursements for seconded employees from foreign headquarters to Indian subsidiaries will attract a 10-15% tax as 'fees for technical services'. This affects several Big Four consultancy firms and MNCs, potentially increasing the cost of hiring international talent. Companies are now reviewing their global secondment agreements.

articles.headline: BSH Repositions Bosch for India’s Entry-Level Consumer Market
articles.summary: BSH Home Appliances is shifting its strategy in India by introducing entry-level Bosch products at lower price points. The German manufacturer aims to challenge market leaders in the washing machine and refrigerator segments by localizing 90% of production. Previously focused on the premium segment, the brand will now target value-conscious urban consumers to gain market share.

articles.headline: Passenger Vehicle Sales Projected to Rise 4-6% by FY27
articles.summary: A report by ICRA suggests that passenger vehicle sales in India will see a moderate growth of 4-6% through fiscal year 2027. The growth is expected to be driven by demand for SUVs and electric vehicles, although high interest rates and rising input costs remain concerns. The industry recorded a strong 27% growth in the last fiscal year, fueled by a post-pandemic consumption surge.

articles.headline: India to Host 26,000 High Net Worth Families by 2030
articles.summary: The number of high-net-worth families in India, those with a net worth over $30 million, is expected to grow from 16,000 today to 26,000 by 2030. This growth is fueling the rise of professional family offices that manage multi-generational wealth and direct investments into startups. India currently ranks behind only the US and China in the number of such wealthy households.

articles.headline: IT Stocks Slump as Accenture Forecasts Lower Quarterly Revenue
articles.summary: Indian IT stocks, including Infosys and TCS, saw a significant decline after global major Accenture lowered its revenue growth forecast. Accenture's warning about delayed spending on consulting and digital transformation has sparked fears of a prolonged slowdown in the Indian tech sector. Analysts predict that major firms may report their lowest organic growth in over a decade.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Minutes Highlight Persistence of Food Inflation Concerns
articles.summary: The recently released minutes of the Reserve Bank of India’s Monetary Policy Committee meeting show that members remain concerned about volatile food prices. Deputy Governor Michael Patra emphasized that while core inflation is easing, shocks in the food supply chain could derail the 4% inflation target. The committee maintains a cautious 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.

articles.headline: SBI Funds Management Receives SEBI Approval for Stock Listing
articles.summary: SBI Funds Management, India's second-largest fund house, has received the green light from SEBI for its proposed initial public offering. The IPO will consist of an offer for sale by existing shareholders, including State Bank of India and Amundi. The listing is expected to set a new valuation benchmark for the Indian asset management industry, which currently manages over ₹65 lakh crore.

articles.headline: Mexico Becomes First Team to Reach Copa America Knockouts
articles.summary: Mexico secured their spot in the Copa America knockout stages with a 1-0 victory over South Korea. A crucial mistake by the Korean goalkeeper allowed Romo to score the only goal of the match. Despite intense pressure in the second half, the Mexican defense held firm to become the first team in the tournament to officially progress. They now look to top their group in the final round.

articles.headline: Switzerland Defeats Bosnia 4-1 with Manzambi’s Second-Half Brace
articles.summary: Switzerland secured a dominant 4-1 victory over Bosnia in their latest Euro 2024 group stage match. The highlight was a two-goal performance from super-sub Manzambi, who changed the course of the game after coming off the bench. The win puts Switzerland in a strong position to qualify for the next round, while Bosnia faces a difficult path ahead following two consecutive losses.

articles.headline: Trump Considers Using Private 'Qatar Force One' for Official Travel
articles.summary: Speculation is rising among US officials that Donald Trump may use a Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar for his future presidential travel. The aircraft, dubbed 'Qatar Force One' by critics, is reportedly more modern than the aging VC-25B currently serving as Air Force One. The move has raised questions about protocol and potential security concerns regarding foreign-gifted transport.

articles.headline: Delhi Capitals May Trade Kuldeep Yadav for Rishabh Pant
articles.summary: Speculation is rife in the IPL circuit that Lucknow Super Giants (LSG) is looking to trade star spinner Kuldeep Yadav to Delhi Capitals in exchange for Rishabh Pant. The potential mega-swap comes as teams begin preparations for the 2025 mega-auction. While neither franchise has officially confirmed the move, sources suggest negotiations are in the early stages regarding valuation.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Royals Lead IPL 2024 Points Table as Standings Are Updated
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals lead the IPL 2024 points table with 16 points. Kolkata Knight Riders and Lucknow Super Giants follow with 12 points each, while Royal Challengers Bengaluru sits at the bottom with 6 points. As the league phase continues, teams are focused on total points and net run rates to secure a playoff berth, making every upcoming match critical for the final top-four standings.

articles.headline: Trade deals have opened doors for youth and businesses says Modi
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi stated that recent trade agreements with nearly 40 countries have created new market opportunities for Indian industries and youth. Speaking at a Rozgar Mela, he highlighted the expansion of sectors like infrastructure and manufacturing, which have generated significant employment opportunities.

articles.headline: Voter ID key as Karnataka cleans up guarantee rolls
articles.summary: The Karnataka government will launch a state-wide verification drive to identify and remove bogus beneficiaries from its five guarantee schemes. Starting June 30, officials will conduct door-to-door checks, making Aadhaar-linked bank accounts and mobile numbers mandatory to ensure benefits reach genuine BPL card holders.

articles.headline: Two feared drowned in BWSSB sewage tank in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A massive search operation is underway for two workers who went missing while cleaning a sewage treatment plant tank at Balagere. Activists allege the workers were engaged illegally for manual scavenging without safety equipment. A three-member panel has been ordered to probe the incident and BWSSB's safety SOPs.

articles.headline: Will study before reacting: DKS on TN's resolution against Mekedatu
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar stated the government would review the Tamil Nadu assembly's unanimous resolution opposing the Mekedatu reservoir project before responding. TN argues the project would affect its share of Cauvery water, while Karnataka maintains it is for drinking water and power generation.

articles.headline: At four billion dollars Jio IPO set to be India's biggest
articles.summary: Reliance Industries has filed draft papers for Jio Platforms' IPO, aiming to raise up to $4 billion. This would be the largest public offering in Indian history, surpassing the 2022 LIC IPO. The funds are expected to be used for debt reduction as the company weights its satellite network expansion.

articles.headline: Monsoon deficit at forty percent as reservoir levels dip sharply
articles.summary: India's monsoon deficit has reached 40%, with water storage in 166 major reservoirs falling to 27% of their total capacity. Central Water Commission data reveals that levels in the northern and central regions are particularly concerning, potentially impacting kharif crop sowing and hydropower generation.

articles.headline: 19km Blue line metro set for trial run in October
articles.summary: The KR Pura-Silk Board stretch of the Bengaluru Metro's Blue Line is scheduled for trial runs starting October 2024. Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda confirmed that 90% of the work is complete, though labor shortages due to recent elections had briefly slowed construction progress.

articles.headline: Same state very different odds for a child's survival
articles.summary: New SRS 2024 data reveals sharp intra-state disparities in infant and under-five mortality rates. High-performing states like Kerala and Tamil Nadu still show gaps between tribal belts and urban areas, while Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh continue to record the highest national averages for child mortality.

articles.headline: PM to lead ceremony to commission two warships into Navy
articles.summary: Prime Minister Modi will lead the commissioning of two indigenous warships, Dunagiri (a stealth frigate) and Agray (an anti-submarine vessel), in Kolkata. The ships, built by Garden Reach Shipbuilders, enhance the Navy's capabilities in coastal defense and anti-submarine warfare.

articles.headline: Even remote Himalayan air losing its clean edge per study
articles.summary: A study at Munsyari found that even remote areas of the Himalayas are seeing build-up of harmful gases and secondary organic aerosols. Researchers from ARIES found levels of VOCs and pollutants are higher during spring and autumn, attributed to regional biomass burning and urban pollution inflow.

articles.headline: NTA guardian of paper making process as well as exams
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency has expanded its expert panel to reduce paper-setting risks following the NEET-UG leak controversy. The new system involves a high-security process where no single expert has a complete view of the final paper, aimed at cutting down the value of partial leaks.

articles.headline: Sebi allows easier transfer of shares for dead investors
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has introduced new conduct rules allowing easier share transfers for deceased investors' heirs. For claims up to Rs 10,000 for physical shares and Rs 30,000 for demat shares, procedures have been simplified to reduce the burden of documentation for small investors.

articles.headline: Our India data centre will power global AI work says Meta
articles.summary: Meta plans to establish its first AI-enabled data centre in India to serve global markets. Vice-president Santosh Janardhan stated the facility will focus on power-efficient technologies to support Meta's networking of global servers, leveraging India's engineering talent and growing digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: Smriti Mandhana makes T20I history with record six hundred fours
articles.summary: India's vice-captain Smriti Mandhana became the first player in T20 international history across men's and women's cricket to hit 600 fours. The milestone came during India's 95-run victory over the Netherlands, where she scored a fluent 85 to lead the team to a massive total.

articles.headline: Scientists discover new insect species in expedition to Angola
articles.summary: An international research team discovered dozens of previously unknown insect species in Angola's Okavango region. Finds include a crab spider that fluoresces under UV light and new species of dragonflies and butterflies, highlighting the biodiversity of Africa's major river sources.

articles.headline: Delhi HC upholds curbs on Telegram amid NEET row
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court upheld the Centre's temporary ban on Telegram, citing its use by cheating networks in the NEET (UG), 2026 controversy. The court found the government followed IT Act Section 69A, including emergency powers, and confirmed "information" includes software. The ban was proportionate given content takedowns were ineffective.

articles.headline: T.N. Assembly unanimously opposes dam at Mekedatu
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Assembly unanimously adopted a resolution opposing Karnataka's proposal to construct a balancing reservoir across the Cauvery at Mekedatu. Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay moved the resolution, citing Karnataka's unilateral attempt without concurrence from basin states or Union government approval, violating tribunal awards.

articles.headline: Satheesan banks on ‘dream projects’ to transform Kerala
articles.summary: Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan tabled the 2026-27 budget, highlighting UDF's 'dream projects' like transforming Kerala into a port city and aviation hub, 'Land Reforms 2.0', and 'Kerala Knowledge Valley'. The budget allocates ₹600 crore for free travel for women, ₹400 crore for Mission Samudra, and ₹100 crore for a health city.

articles.headline: Security up for CJP’s protest at Jantar Mantar today
articles.summary: Security measures, including CCTV cameras, body-worn cameras, and videographers, have been intensified in Delhi for the Cockroach Janta Party’s (CJP) protest at Jantar Mantar on Saturday. Traffic diversions are possible, but metro services are expected to be unaffected.

articles.headline: AAP alleges corruption in tenders under BJP govt.
articles.summary: AAP Delhi chief Saurabh Bharadwaj alleged corruption in tenders issued since the BJP took power in Delhi, citing examples like tent houses for Kanwar Yatra and hospital supplies. The BJP refuted the claims.

articles.headline: Cong. forms panel to run its communications centre
articles.summary: The Delhi Congress constituted a three-member committee to manage, coordinate, monitor, and strengthen communication for its ‘Congress Connect Centre’ outreach initiative.

articles.headline: CM notifies winter air quality framework, outlines curbs
articles.summary: Delhi government notified a ‘Proactive Winter Air Quality Management Framework’ for November-February. Measures, expected from November, include fuel supply only to vehicles with valid PUC certificates, restrictions on non-BS VI commercial vehicles from outside Delhi, double parking charges, and staggered office timings for 50% physical attendance.

articles.headline: City gets first police station only for women, POCSO cases
articles.summary: Lieutenant-Governor Taranjit Singh Sandhu inaugurated Delhi’s first police station exclusively for crimes against women and children in Subzi Mandi. With 60% female personnel, including the SHO and ACP, it aims to create a comfortable environment for complainants, offering counselling and conducting awareness programs.

articles.headline: Woman, son die as parked car rolls into Lahaul gorge
articles.summary: A 26-year-old woman, Tanu Kumari, and her 11-month-old son, Daksh Kumar, from Gurugram, died in Himachal Pradesh’s Lahaul and Spiti district. Their parked car rolled backward and plunged into a gorge near Gramphu while the family was on a holiday trip.

articles.headline: A.P. Congress leaders trek to Tirumala shrine on Rahul’s birthday
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Congress leaders, led by AICC member Sunkara Padmasree, trekked to Tirupati on Rahul Gandhi’s 56th birthday. They prayed for his well-being and sought Lord Venkateswara’s blessings for his efforts to restore democracy and the party’s victory in the next State election.

articles.headline: BJP calls Karnataka leaders to Delhi over ‘cross-voting’ in MLC poll
articles.summary: BJP national president Nitin Nabin summoned Karnataka State chief B.Y. Vijayendra and other leaders to Delhi on June 23 over alleged “cross-voting” in the recent Legislative Council election. The Congress won five of seven seats, BJP two. A fact-finding panel was formed, and the State leadership might be revamped.

articles.headline: Taking part in Yoga Day not mandatory, Bengal govt. tells HC
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court disposed of a petition challenging the West Bengal government's notification asking State employees to participate in International Yoga Day. The State clarified that participation was “not mandatory” and no punitive action would be taken for non-attendance. PM Modi will lead a session in Kolkata.

articles.headline: Bihar to bear cost of bringing back migrant workers’ remains
articles.summary: The Bihar government will now bear the cost of transporting the remains of migrant workers who die outside the State or abroad. The ex-gratia amount for dependents of deceased migrant workers has also been doubled from ₹2,00,000 to ₹4,00,000.

articles.headline: Eight killed in lightning strikes across Jharkhand
articles.summary: At least eight people, including two women and a two-year-old boy, were killed in lightning strikes across six districts of Jharkhand. Hazaribag district reported three deaths. The victims included a 30-year-old woman, a 15-year-old girl, and a 13-year-old girl.

articles.headline: NEET aspirant dies in Indore; probe under way, say police
articles.summary: A 21-year-old NEET aspirant, Avantika Maurya, died after "falling" from the third floor of a building in Indore, Madhya Pradesh, under "suspicious circumstances." Police have launched an investigation. Her family stated she was disheartened by the recent question paper leak and the upcoming re-examination.

articles.headline: Compulsory three-language policy not viable in Nagaland, say CBSE schools
articles.summary: Principals of 19 CBSE-affiliated schools in Nagaland wrote to Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, citing difficulties in implementing the compulsory three-language policy, especially Sanskrit. They highlighted Nagaland's linguistic diversity, with English serving as the lingua franca, and urged a special exemption or flexible framework.

articles.headline: BJP worker killed in Howrah: Suvendu promises law to seize properties of accused
articles.summary: BJP worker Prasanta Dey was killed in Howrah, West Bengal, after a confrontation over alleged corruption. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced his government would introduce a Bill to auction properties of accused in such crimes, offering ₹9 lakh compensation and a government job to Dey's wife. Eight arrests made, case handed to CID.

articles.headline: Maker of Battlefield says his film related to present-day conflict in Manipur
articles.summary: Manipuri filmmaker Borun Thokchom's 80-minute anti-war documentary, Battlefield, revisiting WWII battles of Imphal and Kohima, will screen at the Mumbai International Film Festival. Thokchom states the film, though about events 8 decades ago, is "very much related" to Manipur's present-day ethnic conflict.

articles.headline: Kuki people boarding flights from Imphal a positive sign: CM
articles.summary: Manipur CM Yumnam Khemchand Singh stated Kuki community members traveling to Imphal for flights is a positive sign for the conflict-scarred state. He urged unity and dialogue among the 36 communities for development and peace, noting the economy suffered from violence.

articles.headline: Uproar over defacing of Ambedkar’s statue in U.P.
articles.summary: Jajouli village in U.P.'s Kaushambi district was tense after Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's statue was vandalized. Dalit villagers protested, demanding strict action. Police reinstalled the statue and promised action, stationing personnel in the area.

articles.headline: The pressures of counting India
articles.summary: Census enumerators face challenges like heat, connectivity, and safety during India's first fully digital Census. Field workers, mostly teachers, report hostility, denied entry, and pressure from superiors to edit data, especially on sanitation and water access, affecting data accuracy and raising concerns about real-time monitoring and reporting.

articles.headline: SC refuses urgent hearing on plea to defer NEET retest
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declined to grant an urgent hearing for a plea challenging the NTA's decision to conduct the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination on June 21 for 22 lakh candidates. Aspirants cited heightened stress and anxiety after the original May 3 exam was cancelled due to a paper leak.

articles.headline: Pakistan is an example of a ‘Frankenstein state’, India says at UN
articles.summary: India slammed Pakistan at the UN, calling it a “Frankenstein state” shocked when its “own monster bites back,” accusing Islamabad of hosting, training, and deploying terrorists. India's Anupama Singh rejected Pakistan's allegations on Jammu & Kashmir, stating denial of freedoms there is met with "bullets and brutality."

articles.headline: Extraordinary level of opacity, says Congress leader on Nicobar project
articles.summary: Jairam Ramesh termed Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav’s defence of the Great Nicobar Island Project "disappointing." Ramesh accused the Ministry of "extraordinary level of non-transparency" by withholding reports and studies on environmental safeguards, questioning the adequacy of impact assessments and the secrecy around the HPC report.

articles.headline: A nation capable of meeting its defence needs can move forward confidently: Rajnath
articles.summary: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh inaugurated a 10,000-tonne aluminium extrusion press at Ordnance Factory Ambajhari, Nagpur. He stated a nation meeting its defence needs moves forward confidently, emphasising indigenous manufacturing in defence, aerospace, missiles, and aviation. The project aims to strengthen India's self-reliance in critical sectors.

articles.headline: Palestine envoy seeks medical assistance for Gaza, West Bank
articles.summary: The Palestinian Authority appealed to India for urgent medical assistance for Gaza and the West Bank, citing the healthcare sector's "state of collapse" after three years of Israeli military campaigns. Ambassador Abdullah M. Abu Shawesh highlighted critical shortages of essential medicines, including 50 cancer drugs at zero stock.

articles.headline: SC directs tribunal to hear lawyer’s plea in SIR row
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed an appellate tribunal to expeditiously decide the appeal of 75-year-old Murshidabad lawyer Yean Ali, challenging the deletion of his name from West Bengal's electoral rolls. The Bench noted a panel of former judges exists for such disputes and asked for a decision within two months.

articles.headline: Rebel Trinamool MPs must be disqualified: Abhishek
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress leader Abhishek Banerjee called for the disqualification of 20 Lok Sabha MPs who declared a merger with Nationalist Citizens Party of India (NCPI). He argued that per the Tenth Schedule, two-thirds of the political party, not just legislature, must agree to a merger, accusing them of disrespecting the Constitution.

articles.headline: Trinamool faces fresh exits, arrests in Bengal
articles.summary: Six weeks after election defeat, Trinamool Congress is seeing continued desertions and arrests in West Bengal. Former Minister Jyoti Priya Mallick resigned citing health, as did district president Narendranath Chakraborty. Former MLA Ramendu Sinharoy was arrested in Karnataka over multiple allegations.

articles.headline: Uddhav offers to step down while Shinde hints at more defections
articles.summary: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief Uddhav Thackeray emotionally offered to resign if leaders believed allegations against him for aligning with Congress, condemning BJP's "dirty politics" and "Operation Lotus." Deputy CM Eknath Shinde hinted at more defections, stating the public "has only seen a trailer" of political changes.

articles.headline: Parliamentary panel seeks clarity from govt. on future engagement with Pakistan
articles.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on External Affairs questioned Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri on future engagement with Pakistan, including Prime Minister Modi's attendance at the SCO summit. Ministry officials stated "normal neighbourly relations" require an atmosphere free of violence, noting Track-II diplomacy continues.

articles.headline: Attempt to tarnish image: CPI(ML) on ‘cross-voting’
articles.summary: CPI(ML) Liberation general secretary Dipankar Bhattacharya protested against "malicious propaganda" and attempts to "tarnish our party’s image" following allegations of cross-voting against its MLAs in the Jharkhand Rajya Sabha elections. He affirmed the party's commitment to the INDIA bloc.

articles.headline: Four glacial lakes in Arunachal have expanded in a decade: study
articles.summary: A satellite-based study by Suhora Technologies found four of five glacial lakes in Arunachal Pradesh's Tawang district have expanded over the last decade, with Sanhapo Lake showing rapid growth (10 hectares). This raises concerns about Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs), stressing continuous monitoring.

articles.headline: Sickle cell anaemia to be eradicated by 2047: Murmu
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu announced India would eradicate sickle cell anaemia well before the 2047 target. Speaking in Omkareshwar, she urged collective efforts, especially among tribal communities, for awareness. Murmu noted the National Mission's key targets for screening 7 crore people have been achieved ahead of schedule.

articles.headline: 3 indigenous naval vessels to be launched by PM tomorrow
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will commission three indigenously-built Indian Navy frontline platforms—Dunagiri, Sanshodhak, and Agray—at Kolkata’s Syama Prasad Mookerjee Port. Designed by the Indian Navy, these vessels, with over 75% indigenous content, boost maritime combat, hydrographic surveying, and anti-submarine warfare capabilities.

articles.headline: Man arrested in Chhattisgarh for brutally assaulting wife
articles.summary: A 44-year-old man was arrested in Chhattisgarh's Korea district for brutally assaulting his wife on June 14. He allegedly tied her, partially disrobed her, shaved her head, blackened her face, and forced her to drink their son’s urine over a purported extramarital affair.

articles.headline: Truth will prevail, says U.P. CM on probe into Ram temple row
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath stated "truth will prevail" after the SIT completed its probe into alleged embezzlement of Ram Temple trust donations. He urged devotees not to be misled by Opposition spreading misinformation to "tarnish" Ayodhya's reputation, accusing them of worshipping Babur instead of Ram.

articles.headline: Culture Secretary Vivek Aggarwal is new FATF Vice-President
articles.summary: Union Culture Secretary Vivek Aggarwal was appointed Vice-President of the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) for 2026-2027. His selection signifies India's enhanced role in global financial governance, anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing, and illicit financial flows. He previously headed India's FATF delegation.

articles.headline: World Bank approves $1.5 bn support for India reform plan
articles.summary: The World Bank approved $1.5 billion financing for India's Development Policy Financing (DPF) Operation. This support targets structural reforms to boost private sector-led job creation and economic growth, aiming to create opportunities for 11 million youth entering the labour market annually.

articles.headline: West Asia impact dominated MPC meeting discourse
articles.summary: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) noted that the prolonged West Asia conflict is the most significant risk to India’s economic outlook, raising concerns about inflation, growth, and financial stability. All members agreed to maintain the repo rate at 5.25% and a neutral stance, adopting a cautious approach.

articles.headline: Nifty IT dips on Accenture revenue guidance
articles.summary: The Nifty IT index slid 3.65% after Accenture Plc. cut its full-year revenue guidance to 3-4% growth (local currency), down from 3-5%, citing macroeconomic uncertainty and a $100 million impact from the West Asia crisis. Infosys slid 6.5%, while TCS, LTM, and Tech Mahindra also declined.

articles.headline: RIL to turn oil into chemicals: Ambani
articles.summary: Reliance Industries CMD Mukesh Ambani announced that RIL will convert all refined oil into chemicals and new materials long-term, aiming to make it the world's leading integrated energy and materials company. This transformation, including New Energy initiatives, will create two lakh green jobs.

articles.headline: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific Ocean
articles.summary: The U.S. military attacked a boat suspected of drug smuggling in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three people. This incident brings the total number of deaths in such U.S. military strikes to at least 211 since the Trump administration began targeting "narcoterrorists."

articles.headline: Colombia investigates ex-President Uribe over paramilitary massacres
articles.summary: The Colombian prosecutor’s office initiated a probe into influential former President Alvaro Uribe. He is being investigated for alleged links to massacres by paramilitary groups during the 1996 and 1997 armed conflict, marking a significant development in the country's efforts to address past violence.

articles.headline: Venezuela govt., Opposition hold talks on ‘democratic transition’
articles.summary: Members of Venezuela’s interim government and Opposition representatives met in Caracas to discuss a roadmap for democratic transition. Opposition figure Dinorah Figuera, returning after eight years abroad, joined talks with National Assembly chief Jorge Rodriguez, as confirmed by the U.S. State Department.

articles.headline: U.S. Vice-President Vance delays trip to Switzerland for Iran talks
articles.summary: U.S. Vice-President J.D. Vance delayed his trip to Switzerland, where he was scheduled for talks following up on the deal to end the war with Iran. The White House spokesperson cited "logistics of these negotiations" as complex and unpredictable for the delay.

articles.headline: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill three: Kyiv
articles.summary: Russia renewed strikes on Ukraine, killing three people, including an eight-year-old girl, Ukrainian officials reported. This comes a day after Ukraine's largest-ever drone attack on Moscow, which also killed an eight-year-old and sparked an oil refinery fire. The Kremlin vowed to "continue" striking Ukraine.

articles.headline: Starmer says he won’t step down after Burnham’s victory
articles.summary: U.K. Labour leader Keir Starmer affirmed he would not resign after Andy Burnham's Makerfield byelection victory, despite a weakening grip on his position. Burnham, a left-leaning challenger, comfortably won with 55% of the vote, calling for Labour to change. Starmer faces pressure for an internal party contest.

articles.headline: ‘All of Lebanon must burn’, says Israeli Minister Ben Gvir
articles.summary: Far-right Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir stated, "All of Lebanon must burn," following the deaths of four Israeli soldiers. He added, "For every tear shed by an Israeli mother, a thousand Lebanese mothers must weep," urging Israel to "go wild" and "open the gates of hell."

articles.headline: Italy’s top diplomat cancels U.S. trip as Meloni slams Trump’s claim
articles.summary: Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani cancelled his U.S. trip after President Donald Trump claimed Premier Giorgia Meloni "begged" for a photo-op at the G7 summit. Meloni called Trump's claims "completely fabricated" and "offensive," asserting, "Italy and I do not beg."

articles.headline: How pitches are laid, how they age, and why it’s both art and science
articles.summary: A cricket pitch is a blend of clay, water, and grass, its behavior governed by these components and environmental factors. Curators select clay types, use heavy rollers, and manage moisture through deep saturation. Grass reinforces soil and influences ball friction. Pitches deteriorate over time, with decay depending on clay type and weather, impacting game dynamics.

articles.headline: Hot-seat shuffle: Alonso, Iraola, Maresca sign up for big-club roulette
articles.summary: Xabi Alonso, Andoni Iraola, and Enzo Maresca have joined Chelsea, Liverpool, and Manchester City as new managers, respectively. Their interconnected destinies involve high expectations. Alonso faces challenges reviving Chelsea after a turbulent season, Iraola must resuscitate Liverpool without Salah, and Maresca fills Guardiola’s shoes amidst City’s financial charges.

articles.headline: Super subs fire Switzerland past Bosnia and close in on last 32 spot
articles.summary: Super substitutes Johan Manzambi and Ruben Vargas led Switzerland to a 4-1 victory over 10-man Bosnia-Herzegovina in Los Angeles. The late goal rush gave the Swiss a strong chance to qualify for the FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout round as one of the top two teams in Group B.

articles.headline: Six-star Canada hammers Qatar into submission
articles.summary: Jonathan David scored three goals, leading Canada to a dominant 6-0 victory over Qatar. This win marked Canada's first World Cup match victory and almost secured its spot in the knockout round. Qatar was reduced to nine players due to red cards in the chaotic match.

articles.headline: Alleyne’s heroics hand West Indies thrilling victory over Scotland
articles.summary: Aaliyah Alleyne's three-wicket burst helped West Indies secure a thrilling seven-run victory over Scotland in a Women's T20 World Cup match in Leeds. West Indies scored 153/6, with Taylor hitting 47 n.o., while Scotland was restricted to 146. Taylor was named Player of the Match.

articles.headline: Prema replaces injured Shreyanka in India’s T20 World Cup squad
articles.summary: Leg-spinner Prema Rawat has replaced Shreyanka Patil in India’s T20 Women’s World Cup squad. Patil injured her ankle during India’s match against the Netherlands and was ruled out of the tournament. The Event Technical Committee approved the replacement.

articles.headline: Hydration break appears to be affecting the outcome of contests
articles.summary: Hydration breaks, introduced in the FIFA World Cup to protect players from heat, seem to influence match outcomes. 53 of 89 goals so far were scored after these breaks, with 20 within 10 minutes of restart. Coaches are recognizing tactical possibilities. The breaks also provide lucrative commercial opportunities for broadcasters.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka-A to meet India-A in the final
articles.summary: Sri Lanka-A will face India-A in the tri-nation tournament final. The host team secured its spot by beating Afghanistan-A by 103 runs, with Avishka Fernando scoring a century and Dulaj Samuditha taking five wickets. Captain Sahan Arachchige urged his side to focus on the trophy.

articles.headline: Shree Charani — a great find for the Women in Blue
articles.summary: Left-arm spinner Shree Charani has become an integral part of the Indian women's cricket team, showing impressive form since her T20I debut a year ago. She has taken 62 international wickets, is ranked No. 2 in ICC T20I bowlers, and is praised for her control and variations.

articles.headline: Jane and Jill — the pioneers who created a rich legacy
articles.summary: Twins Jane and Jill Powell, former England cricketers, reflect on their pioneering careers and the growth of women's cricket. Jane, a former England captain, led in a World Cup final and was Yorkshire's first female president. They noted the lack of recognition and money in their era compared to today's game.

articles.headline: Nicholls puts Kiwis on top
articles.summary: Henry Nicholls scored an unbeaten century, helping New Zealand boost its lead to 352 runs against England on day three of the second Test at the Oval. New Zealand is currently 252/3 in its second innings.

articles.headline: Dominant India eyes series sweep against Afghanistan
articles.summary: India, having clinched the ODI series against Afghanistan, aims for a 3-0 sweep in the final game at Chepauk. The team will likely experiment with bench strength, with young pacers like Gurnoor Brar and Prince Yadav impressing. Afghanistan seeks improved batting performance.

articles.headline: India finishes runner-up in World Team snooker c’ship
articles.summary: India lost 2-3 to host China 'A' in the final of the World Team snooker championship in Guangdong, finishing as runner-up. Pankaj Advani secured two wins, but China 'A' ultimately clinched the title.

articles.headline: Macau Open: Ashmita storms into semifinals, Anmol out
articles.summary: Ashmita Chaliha advanced to the women’s singles semifinals at the Macau Open Super 300 tournament, defeating Korea’s Kim Min Sun 21-16, 21-18. However, compatriot Anmol Kharb was eliminated after a quarterfinal defeat to Han Qian Xi of China.

articles.headline: Prachi leads the way as India puts up a good show
articles.summary: Prachi secured a 4-1 victory against Paris Olympics bronze medallist Shih Yi Wu of Taiwan, confirming multiple medals for India at the Boxing World Cup 2026 (Stage 2). Other Indian boxers like Minakshi, Nikhil, and Deepak also advanced to the semifinals.

articles.headline: Yuvraj to join Delhi Capitals as batting coach
articles.summary: Former white-ball stalwart Yuvraj Singh is set to join Delhi Capitals as its batting coach for the 2027 season, according to IPL sources. This move will bring his extensive experience to the franchise.

articles.headline: Manika rails against Asiad exclusion
articles.summary: India’s top women’s paddler Manika Batra sought intervention from the Sports Ministry and IOA after her exclusion from the Asian Games table tennis squad. The 2018 bronze medallist stated her omission was "deeply disheartening" due to how selection criteria were interpreted.

articles.headline: Thanya swims to new mark in 400m medley
articles.summary: Thanya Shadakshari broke an eight-year-old record in the women’s 400m medley at the National aquatics championships in Ahmedabad, clocking 4:56.78. Tanishi Gupta also set a new national mark twice in the women's 100m butterfly.

articles.headline: Sang-uk clinches men’s sabre gold
articles.summary: South Korean Oh Sang-uk won gold in the men’s sabre event on the opening day of the 26th Senior Asian Fencing Championships in New Delhi. China’s Luo Shaotong took silver, while two South Koreans and one Japanese fencer shared bronze.

articles.headline: IOA and NCF organise Athletes’ Forum
articles.summary: The second IOA Athletes’ Forum gathered over 75 active and retired athletes from 30+ sports federations. It launched the Clean Sport Campaign and focused on providing practical insights and professional guidance for athletes' journeys and post-competitive careers.

articles.headline: Reliance Foundation launch movement-based activities and community sports sessions
articles.summary: The International Olympic Committee, Abhinav Bindra Foundation, and Reliance Foundation launched ‘Let’s Move’ initiative in India. Starting on Olympic Day (June 23), it aims to encourage young people to be active through sporting activities and community sports sessions, engaging 1,800 Anganwadis and 44,000 children.

articles.headline: Iniyan wins 360 One Global Chess Masters title
articles.summary: GM Pa. Iniyan won the 360 One Global Chess Masters crown, accumulating 7.5 points from 9 rounds. The 23-year-old from Erode, a top seed, secured six wins and three draws in the tournament.

articles.headline: India loses to Germany
articles.summary: India lost 1-2 to reigning champion Germany in an FIH Pro League match. Germany secured the win with Jakob Brilla scoring a penalty corner in the 56th minute after the teams were goalless at halftime.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms Files DRHP for India's Largest ₹40,000 Crore IPO
articles.summary: Jio Platforms (JPL) filed a DRHP with SEBI for India's largest IPO, valued at ₹35,000-₹40,000 crore (approx. $4 billion). The issue of 27 crore fresh shares aims to repay ₹27,500 crore debt of subsidiary Reliance Jio Infocomm and fund digital/telecom growth, potentially valuing the company at over $150 billion.

articles.headline: SEBI Reinstates Open Market Stock Buybacks via Exchanges from August 1
articles.summary: SEBI approved reintroduction of open-market buybacks via stock exchanges from August 1, following eased tax treatment. Buybacks must be announced electronically and completed within 66 working days through the normal trading window. This provides flexibility for capital management and ensures transparency. SEBI also relaxed mutual fund borrowing norms, adopted a new board member code, and approved changes for municipal bonds and quick securities transfer.

articles.headline: Jio Platforms Explores LEO Satellite Constellation for Broadband, AI Backbone
articles.summary: Jio Platforms is developing a sovereign Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellation and building ground station infrastructure to provide high-speed satellite broadband across India, competing with Starlink and OneWeb. Reliance Industries CMD Mukesh Ambani announced this, stating Jio demonstrated JioSpaceFiber and seeks GMPCS approvals for an end-to-end ecosystem. Jio is also building India's sovereign AI backbone.

articles.headline: Supreme Court: Right to Safe Footpaths a Fundamental Right Under Article 21
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declared the right to walk on safe, unobstructed, and well-maintained footpaths a fundamental right under Article 21, prioritizing pedestrians over vehicles. It directed civic authorities to ensure all roads have dedicated, unencroached footpaths, noting citizens can sue for violations. Justice Narasimha stressed a statutory framework is needed, citing a case where a child was killed.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Upholds Telegram Channel Ban Over NEET-UG Leaks, Citing Misinformation
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court declined to lift the temporary ban on specific Telegram channels accused of circulating leaked NEET-UG papers and misinformation. The court deemed it a "least restrictive measure" and necessary emergency action, stating the government followed due process. Telegram is required to cooperate with investigators by sharing user data linked to the leaks.

articles.headline: Israel, Hezbollah Agree to Truce in Southern Lebanon After Deadly Clashes
articles.summary: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to a halt in heavy fighting in southern Lebanon, mediated by the US, Qatar, and Iran, following clashes that killed 47 in Lebanon and four Israeli soldiers. Israeli PM Netanyahu warned of severe retaliation if the truce is violated, with Israeli forces remaining in a temporary security zone. The accord faces threats from Lebanon and delays planned U.S.-Iran talks.

articles.headline: Co-host Mexico First to Reach World Cup Knockout Stages with 1-0 Win
articles.summary: Co-host Mexico became the first team to reach the 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stages with a 1-0 win over South Korea in Guadalajara. Luis Romo scored the decisive 56th-minute goal after a goalkeeping error, with stopper Raul Rangel making crucial late saves. Meanwhile, Canada edged closer to the last 32 after beating Qatar.

articles.headline: Neeraj Chopra Finishes Fourth in Doha Diamond League Season Opener
articles.summary: Olympic javelin champion Neeraj Chopra finished fourth in his season opener at the Doha Diamond League after recovering from a minor groin injury. He achieved a best throw of 85.69m. Sri Lanka’s Rumesh Pathirage won the event with 88.68m, with Grenada's Anderson Peters placing second with 88.38m and Curtis Thompson third.

articles.headline: Home Minister Launches 'Abhigyan' App for Instant Police Fingerprint Verification
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah launched 'Abhigyan', a smartphone app for police to instantly verify criminal records. It links portable fingerprint scanners to the National Automated Fingerprint Identification System (NAFIS) database of 1.3 crore suspects/convicts, enabling real-time identification for ground-level policing. Shah emphasized continuous database enrichment and time-bound justice.

articles.headline: Doctor Remanded in Custody for Domestic Help's Murder; Mental Illness Cited
articles.summary: Delhi court remanded dermatologist Dr. Manish Gupta to two-day police custody for the murder of his 58-year-old domestic help, Mina Halder, who sustained three stab wounds. A neighbor found Gupta disoriented, confessing "I have done it." Police seek custody to probe co-conspirators. The victim's family alleged police rushed cremation, which police deny. Gupta reportedly suffers from mental illnesses.
